reasoning: The probability of a $100M+ US movie released before the end of 2025 being acknowledged as significantly written/assisted by AI is extremely low. First, the 2023 WGA strike resulted in strict protections against AI, and industry stigma makes it highly unlikely any writer would admit to using it for a 'significant portion' of a blockbuster script. Second, US Copyright Office rulings indicate AI-generated text is not copyrightable, meaning studios financing $100M+ budgets will explicitly prohibit significant AI use in scripts to protect their intellectual property. Third, the production timelines for $100M+ movies usually run 2-4 years, meaning 2025 releases were likely written in 2022-2024, when AI was either less capable or the strikes made its use highly radioactive. Missing major web search tools to check for specific anomalies, structural fundamentals strongly dictate a 'No' outcome. I estimate this at the floor probability of 2%.
